title: Quantum-metric Bloch oscillations in weakly inhomogeneous electric fields
authors: M. Maneesh Kumar, Md Kaif Faiyaz, Sayan Sarkar, Amit Agarwal
abstract: Geometric analogs of Bloch oscillations studied so far have relied on Berry curvature. We show that a weakly inhomogeneous electric field adds a distinct quantum-metric term to semiclassical wavepacket dynamics, generating an oscillatory real-space contribution even when the Berry curvature vanishes. The associated transport response comprises an intrinsic and a scattering-time-dependent part. In the regime studied, the latter can dominate and approach finite saturation at high field when the relative field inhomogeneity is held fixed. A tilted Dirac model illustrates the mechanism. Realistic platforms will likely require synthetically engineered superlattices, with a finite quantum metric and an adequate band gap.
summary: The paper demonstrates that an inhomogeneous electric field can drive Bloch-like oscillations through the quantum metric, even in the absence of Berry curvature. It shows that this quantum-metric-driven current behaves differently from conventional Bloch oscillations, specifically approaching a finite saturation in high fields. This provides a new mechanism for observing band-geometric effects in synthetic lattices.
main_problem: Investigating whether a band-geometric analog of Bloch oscillations can exist in systems where the Berry curvature vanishes by utilizing the quantum metric.
main_result: A weakly inhomogeneous electric field introduces a distinct quantum-metric term to semiclassical dynamics, generating oscillatory real-space motion even when Berry curvature is zero.
method: Extended semiclassical equations of motion for wavepacket dynamics and the semiclassical Boltzmann equation using a relaxation-time approximation.
model_or_system: A tilted Dirac model used as an illustrative system, considering electrons in a periodic potential subject to a weakly inhomogeneous electric field.
key_observables: Real-space position (drift and oscillatory components), quantum-metric-induced current (longitudinal and transverse), and differential conductance.
important_parameters: Berry curvature, quantum metric, electric field gradient, static electric field, and chemical potential.
main_assumptions: The electric field varies slowly over the wavepacket size (weak-gradient approximation) and the semiclassical wavepacket approximation is valid.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows schematics of Berry-curvature-driven vs. quantum-metric-induced Bloch oscillations; Figure 2 shows quantum-metric components in a tilted Dirac model; Figure 3 compares the scaling of quantum-metric current (saturation) vs. conventional Bloch current (negative differential conductance).
paper_structure: The paper identifies the problem of Berry-curvature-independent oscillations, derives modified semiclassical equations of motion including field gradients, applies these to a tilted Dirac model, calculates transport signatures (current and conductance), and discusses experimental platforms like moire superlattices.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

(b) Quantum-metric-induced Bloch oscillations in the presence of an inhomogeneous electric field. The resulting motion can have components both parallel and perpendicular to the ap- plied field.
